key_insight: The paper extends a greedy-optimization plus alternating-direction data-driven solver (GO-ADM) to geometrically exact nonlinear beam analysis and adds a penalty-based thermomechanical consistency enforcement.
engineering_relevance: This enables structural analysis directly from constitutive data or synthetic stress-strain fields, reducing dependence on hand-fit material models for beam structures.
method: Initialize the data-driven solve using a conventional finite element analysis of the same beam structure.
Use GO-ADM to combine greedy optimization with alternating direction updates for the discrete stress-strain matching.
Add a weak penalty term to enforce thermomechanical consistency in the computed discrete fields.
evidence_signal: The authors report numerical examples for single- and multi-member beams showing that the penalty term produces thermomechanically consistent fields and that GO-ADM better approximates the global optimum than standard ADM.
watch_item: The approach relies on high-quality initialization/data and the penalty strength must be chosen carefully to avoid degrading solution accuracy or consistency.
paper_title: A data-driven solving strategy based on a greedy optimization algorithm for the analysis of nonlinear beam structures
